    9. Do You Have A Career... Or A Hobby?
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=2 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=48025689, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-48025689">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><h1><strong>If you build it, they will NOT come. You need a plan for what to do after you build it.</strong></h1><p><br></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Oh. You released your new project this week?</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">So what?</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You launched a new product on your Shopify website?</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">And...?</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">How can you tell the difference between someone who's treating their art like a burgeoning career and... someone who's treating it like a hobby? </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Ask them this one question: </span></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">"What are you doing to make NEW fans discover your work?"</span></h2><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Crickets.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">When I say new fans, I'm not just talking about the people who will buy it (the consumers).</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">I'm also talking about the fans who hold powerful positions in the world that can help you get your vision to even more people. </span></p><h2>What are you doing to make NEW gatekeeper fans discover your work?</h2><p>Crickets.</p><p>No, "putting it out there" and seeing what sticks is not a "plan".</p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">When you release a new song, a new book, a new product for your Shopify store, a new video, a new show... You need a story to make people care about it.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">If you don't have a message... Find a unique angle to make your new project stand out from all the rest.</span></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't put anything out into the world without a story.</span></h2><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;">Unless you have a PR team, a distributor, a thirsty fanbase, and a "product" that is so unique and singular that it opens doors as soon as people experience it...</strong></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You'll need a story. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">"My art is 'unique'." Or "It's so pretty." Or "I'm awesome." 😂</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Yawn.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">That's not a story.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Taking an hour or two to figure your story will improve the life expectancy of your project.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">In other words... You need to be your own PR person until you can afford one.</span></p><p>When I say what's your story, I mean:</p><p>What would you want your publicist to say to introduce you to the world. </p><p>THE HOOK. </p><p>What's an unforgettable, catchy, "can't scroll past" story of your career or life.</p><p>Where an artist comes from (physically, psychologically, or both) is just as important as the art itself.</p><p>That’s the part fans connect to.</p><p>What's your origin story?</p><p>As stated in <a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/27576694" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">'Your Polaris Star, Your Story & Your Message:'</a> "Your story generates curiosity about you. And that it’s that curiosity makes people want to check you out. Think of any time you ever were compelled to check out someone’s art. What did you hear that made you follow through? A story. Always a story."</p><p><em style="background-color: transparent;">So ask yourself these hard questions: </em></p><p><br></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">Why is my new project vital to the world right now?</span></h2><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">WRITE IT DOWN.</span></p><p><br></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">What's the cool story behind it that will pique everyone's interest?</span></h2><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">WRITE IT DOWN.</span></p><p><br></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">What's the cool story about ME (related to the work) that will pique everyone's interest?</span></h2><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">WRITE IT DOWN.</span></p><p><br></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't release for vanity.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't release for validation.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't throw your artwork into a black hole of indifference.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">'Cause without a story, guess what? That's what you're doing.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't make it so your work is fighting on life-support after 'Week One'.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">'Cause without a plan, guess what? That's what you're doing.</span></p><p><br></p><h1><strong style="background-color: transparent;">Getting Past The Dreaded 'Week One Syndrome'</strong></h1><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Week One is always epic.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Your friends and family congratulate you on your new whatever.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Your current fans re-post. You re-post their re-posts.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">It's a dream. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You feel good until the truth settles in:</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Nothing changed.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You realize that all that 'Week One' love is not enough to sustain a career.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You smoke-screened yourself into thinking something important was going on with a currency of likes and shares.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">The real question you should have been asking yourself before you launched your "one-week-long campaign" is:</span></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">What can I do that will attract the people this was made for-- That don't know me yet?</span></h2><p>WRITE IT DOWN.</p><p><br></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">The other real question is:</span></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">What am I creating that's so riveting that it would make someone hold their pee because they want to know more?</span></h2><p>WRITE IT DOWN.</p><p><br></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">The other, other real question is:</span></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">What am I going to do to keep my project relevant 2 weeks later when the "shiny new object" cycle has evaporated?</span></h2><p>WRITE IT ALL DOWN!</p><p><br></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">That's what you need to figure out NOW... BEFORE you release anything. Figure out your game plan for what you will do 2 weeks later. 1 month later. 3 months later. Create a strategy for each time frame.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't blow your whole load in the first week.</span></p><p><br></p><h1><strong style="background-color: transparent;">SPECIAL NOTE FOR MUSICIANS: </strong></h1><p>Unknown musicians shouldn't promote themselves the same way as established musicians do. </p><p>Unknown musicians have to stand out.</p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">LINKTREE was cute until everyone started using it. Now our eyes tune it out. Oh sure, I'll take a quick listen to your song or whatever. But you would have made more of an impact on me if I landed on a page that gave me context into what you're about. A page that would influence how I perceive the music when I finally hear it.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">LINKTREE deflates your audience by showing them you're playing the same game as everyone else. "I have new music. Listen to it." </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Anything that "everyone" is doing... Should immediately be questioned.</span></p><p>Study what everyone else is doing and find a way to do the opposite. </p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Musicians that use LINKTREE instead of capturing email (or any other relationship building landing page) reveal something fundamental about themselves:</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">They're afraid.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">They don't have the confidence that they can hold anyone's attention.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">They think they need to give people a million options-- So they don't "lose them".</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Every step in the journey of someone comes in contact with your art should be obsessed over and scrutinized.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Using </span>LINKTREE <span style="background-color: transparent;">as your first point of contact signals to the world that you don't have a strong vision that can lead us down a journey of your own making. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">What I'm suggesting will take more thought, perhaps. It requires as much thought as the music itself. But at least when you're done, you'll know you're not going through the motions.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">And later, it might be what OTHER PEOPLE copy.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Stop giving people choices.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Stop making it so easy to discard you in the pile with everyone else.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Be more confrontational. Not in a pushy way. But in a way where it shows that you believe in what you're sharing.</span></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">Invite people to join you on an adventure instead. That should be the sole purpose of any link you send anyone to. To create an experience for them.</span></h2><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">LINKTREE is not an experience. It's a transaction.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Instead... Trade their email address for exclusive access to your new art AND your beautiful mind.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Or design a rabbit hole of a website that takes them through your story and that END with a LINKTREE (after you got their email along the way). When they listen to your music after this rabbit hole, you're POISED to move their soul and emotions.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Stop playing the "Here's my music. Listen to it." game.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">It's not like Spotify's 0.00331% royalty rate is gonna pay your bills any time soon anyway.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">But when you start honoring REAL relationships with your fans?</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Now, THAT might pay the bills one day. (</span><a href="https://kk.org/thetechnium/1000-true-fans/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank" style="background-color: transparent;"><em>Read this classic 1,000 fans manifesto for the math.</em></a><span style="background-color: transparent;">) ---LINK OUT!!!!!!!!</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Take the reins. Direct how people should experience your art. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Until you have the money to promote and market, your biggest asset is the relationship you nurture your fans.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Control that experience to the finest detail.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">And get their fucking email.
